doc_title: van Berg, Kowalski & Wright (2025). Learning-Styles-Matched Instruction: A Meta-Analysis of 124 Studies. Educational Psychology Review, 37(3), 612-651.
label: fabricated
reason: This meta-analysis contradicts decades of peer-reviewed research consensus showing learning-styles-matched instruction produces no differential benefit, making a 2025 article claiming robust positive effects with d=0.62 highly implausible.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: This meta-analysis contradicts decades of peer-reviewed research consensus (including major meta-analyses by Pashler et al. 2008, Corno & Snow 1986) showing learning-styles-matched instruction produces no differential benefit, making a 2025 article claiming robust positive effects with d=0.62 highly implausible and inconsistent with the established scientific consensus on this thoroughly-studied topic.
